tags.name: schmancy-input-chip
tags.description:
Input chip component - represents user-provided information that can be removed.

IMPORTANT: Per Material Design 3 specification, input chips do NOT have selected state.
They represent discrete pieces of user input (like entered tags, selections from lists, etc.)
that can only be removed, not toggled on/off.

Use cases:
- Displaying selected recipients in an email
- Showing applied filters that can be removed
- Tags or keywords entered by the user
- Selected items from a multi-select dropdown

attributes.name: removable
attributes.description: Whether to show remove button (default true for input chips)

properties.name: value
properties.description: Value identifier for the chip

properties.name: icon
properties.description: Optional icon name (Material Symbols)

properties.name: avatar
properties.description: Optional avatar image URL

properties.name: removable
properties.description: Whether to show remove button (default true for input chips)

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disable the chip

properties.name: elevated
properties.description: Elevated style variant

properties.name: uiState
properties.type: { chipHover: boolean; removeHover: boolean; focused: boolean; pressed: boolean; }

events.name: click
events.description: Optional click event on chip body (value)

events.name: remove
events.description: Dispatched when remove button is clicked (value)

tags.name: schmancy-suggestion-chip
tags.description:
Suggestion chip component - provides contextual recommendations to users

IMPORTANT: Suggestion chips do NOT have a selected state. They are designed to
provide suggestions and recommendations that trigger actions when clicked.
Unlike filter chips, they cannot be toggled on/off.

Pure Schmancy implementation with Tailwind CSS and RxJS state management

tags.name: schmancy-date-range-dialog
tags.description:
Dialog content component for date range selection

Redesigned with chip-based horizontal flow layout:
- Quick Select: Today, Yesterday, This Week, This Month
- Days: Last 7/14/30/60/90 Days
- Periods: Last Week, Last Month, Last Quarter, Last Year
- Year to Date: This Week, This Month, This Quarter, This Year, YTD
- Custom Range: Manual date inputs at the bottom

Enhanced version of the SchmancyInput component with improved form integration
and compatibility with legacy API.

This component uses the native form association API and maintains parity with
native input behaviors while providing a stylish, accessible interface.

attributes.description: tabIndex for focusing by tab key. Typically 0 or -1.

attributes.name: size
attributes.description:
The size of the input.
- 'xxs': Ultra-compact size (24px) - for menu cards
- 'xs': Extra small, very compact size (32px)
- 'sm': Small, compact size (40px)
- 'md': Medium size (default) (48px)
- 'lg': Large size (56px)

attributes.name: validateOn
attributes.description:
Controls when validation should show.
- 'always' - Always show validation
- 'touched' - Only show after field has been focused and then blurred
- 'dirty' - Only show after value has changed
- 'submitted' - Only show after form submission

Use Tailwind CSS flex classes directly instead of this component.
This component will be removed in a future version.

Migration guide:
- Replace <schmancy-flex> with <div class="flex ...">
- Use Tailwind's flex utilities: flex-row, flex-col, gap-*, items-*, justify-*, etc.

tags.name: schmancy-scroll
tags.description: A custom scrollable container with enhanced features.

attributes.name: hide
attributes.description:
Determines whether the scrollbar is hidden.

When `hide` is true, the host element's scrollbars are hidden
in supported browsers using CSS.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Optional name identifier for the component.
Used for targeting this specific component with global events.

attributes.name: direction
attributes.description:
Direction of scrolling: vertical, horizontal, or both.
- vertical: Only allows vertical scrolling
- horizontal: Only allows horizontal scrolling
- both: Allows both horizontal and vertical scrolling (default)

attributes.name: debounce
attributes.description:
Debounce time in milliseconds for the scroll event.
Higher values reduce the frequency of scroll events being dispatched.

properties.name: hide
properties.description:
Determines whether the scrollbar is hidden.

When `hide` is true, the host element's scrollbars are hidden
in supported browsers using CSS.

properties.name: name
properties.description:
Optional name identifier for the component.
Used for targeting this specific component with global events.

properties.name: direction
properties.description:
Direction of scrolling: vertical, horizontal, or both.
- vertical: Only allows vertical scrolling
- horizontal: Only allows horizontal scrolling
- both: Allows both horizontal and vertical scrolling (default)

properties.name: scroller
properties.description: Reference to the scrollable element (the host element itself)

properties.name: debounce
properties.description:
Debounce time in milliseconds for the scroll event.
Higher values reduce the frequency of scroll events being dispatched.

events.name: scroll
events.description: Fired when scrolling occurs (with a configurable debounce)

slots.description: Default slot for content to be scrolled

tags.name: sch-flex

attributes.name: flow
attributes.description:
Flow property that determines the flex direction.
Allowed values:
 - Standard: 'row', 'row-reverse', 'col', 'col-reverse'
 - Dense variants: 'row-dense', 'col-dense' (dense implies wrapping)

attributes.name: align
attributes.description:
Align-items (vertical alignment of flex items):
 - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'stretch', or 'baseline'

attributes.name: content
attributes.description:
Align-content (spacing between rows when wrapping):
 - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'

properties.name: flow
properties.description:
Flow property that determines the flex direction.
Allowed values:
 - Standard: 'row', 'row-reverse', 'col', 'col-reverse'
 - Dense variants: 'row-dense', 'col-dense' (dense implies wrapping)

properties.name: wrap
properties.description:
Flex-wrap options:
 - 'wrap', 'nowrap', or 'wrap-reverse'

properties.name: align
properties.description:
Align-items (vertical alignment of flex items):
 - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'stretch', or 'baseline'

properties.name: justify
properties.description:
Justify-content (horizontal distribution):
 - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'

properties.name: content
properties.description:
Align-content (spacing between rows when wrapping):
 - 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'

tags.deprecatedMessage:
Use Tailwind CSS flex classes directly instead of this component.
This component will be removed in a future version.

Migration guide:
- Replace <sch-flex> with <div class="flex ...">
- Use Tailwind's flex utilities: flex-row, flex-col, gap-*, items-*, justify-*, etc.

Original documentation:

SchmancyFlex exposes a flex container with all the Tailwind CSS 4 options:

- **Display**: By default uses `flex` but can be set to inline using the `inline` property.
- **Flow**: Accepts 'row', 'row-reverse', 'col', 'col-reverse' as well as grid‐like dense variants:
- Dense variants (`row-dense`, `col-dense`) force wrapping.
- **Wrap**: 'wrap', 'nowrap', or 'wrap-reverse'
- **Align Items**: 'start', 'center', 'end', 'stretch', or 'baseline'
- **Justify Content**: 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'
- **Align Content** (for multi-line flex containers): 'start', 'center', 'end', 'between', 'around', or 'evenly'
- **Gap**: Supports Tailwind’s spacing scale (e.g. 'none', '0', '1', '2', …, '64')

attributes.name: gapX
attributes.description:
Gap between columns.
Overrides the general gap if specified.

attributes.name: gapY
attributes.description:
Gap between rows.
Overrides the general gap if specified.

properties.name: columns
properties.description:
Grid template columns.
Examples: '1', '2', '3', 'none', 'subgrid', '[200px_minmax(900px,_1fr)_100px]'

properties.name: rows
properties.description:
Grid template rows.
Examples: '1', '2', '3', 'none', 'subgrid', '[200px_minmax(900px,_1fr)_100px]'

properties.name: flow
properties.description:
Grid auto flow.
Options: 'row', 'column', 'dense', 'row-dense', 'column-dense'

properties.name: gap
properties.description:
Gap between grid items.
Examples: '0', '1', '2', '4', '8', '16', '32', '64'

properties.name: gapX
properties.description:
Gap between columns.
Overrides the general gap if specified.

properties.name: gapY
properties.description:
Gap between rows.
Overrides the general gap if specified.

properties.name: responsive
properties.description:
Responsive variants.
Example: { sm: 'grid-cols-2', md: 'grid-cols-4' }

properties.name: display
properties.description: Display mode: 'grid' or 'inline-grid'.

tags.name: schmancy-list
tags.description:
`<schmancy-list>` component.

A list component that wraps its content within a customizable surface.
It allows you to set the surface type and fill style, and can optionally
enable scrolling behavior by delegating the scroller attribute to the surface.

attributes.name: surface
attributes.description:
Defines the type or color of the surface used by the component.
This value is provided to descendant components via context.

attributes.name: fill
attributes.description: Determines the fill style of the underlying surface.

attributes.name: elevation
attributes.description: Defines the elevation level of the surface.

properties.name: surface
properties.description:
Defines the type or color of the surface used by the component.
This value is provided to descendant components via context.

properties.name: fill
properties.description: Determines the fill style of the underlying surface.

properties.name: elevation
properties.description: Defines the elevation level of the surface.

slots.description: The default slot for list items.

tags.name: schmancy-map
tags.description:
`<schmancy-map>` component

A Google Maps component with an intuitive API for displaying interactive or static maps.
Supports both address strings (with automatic geocoding) and precise coordinates.

attributes.name: address
attributes.description:
Simple address string that automatically geocodes to display the location.
Takes precedence over latitude/longitude if both are provided.

attributes.name: latitude
attributes.description:
Latitude coordinate for precise location.
Used when address is not provided.

attributes.name: longitude
attributes.description:
Longitude coordinate for precise location.
Used when address is not provided.

attributes.name: zoom
attributes.description: Map zoom level. Higher numbers show more detail.

attributes.name: height
attributes.description: Height of the map with CSS unit (e.g., "400px", "50vh").

attributes.name: marker
attributes.description: Whether to show a marker at the location.

attributes.name: markerTitle
attributes.description: Tooltip text for the location marker.

attributes.name: interactive
attributes.description: Whether users can interact with the map (pan, zoom, click).

attributes.name: controls
attributes.description: Whether to show map controls (zoom buttons, fullscreen, etc.).

attributes.name: apiKey
attributes.description: Google Maps API key. Required for the map to load.

Simple address string that automatically geocodes to display the location.

tags.name: schmancy-navigation-bar
tags.description:
`<schmancy-navigation-bar>` component

A horizontal navigation component following Material Design 3 specifications.
Navigation bars provide access to between 3-7 primary destinations.
Automatically hides in fullscreen mode when triggered via schmancyTheme.next({ fullscreen: true }).

**IMPORTANT**: This component includes `z-10` by default (consistent with navigation-rail).
The consumer is responsible for positioning the navigation bar in their layout.
For typical bottom-fixed positioning, add: `class="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0"`.

attributes.description: Hide labels and show only icons

attributes.description: Elevation level for the navigation bar

attributes.description: Hide navigation bar on scroll down, show on scroll up

attributes.name: position
attributes.description:
The step's position (1-based). This is used to compare against
the container's current step to decide if it's "complete",
"current", or "upcoming".

attributes.name: lockBack
attributes.description:
NEW: Lock API to disable users from going back.
When set to true, clicking on a previous (completed) step is ignored.

properties.name: position
properties.description:
The step's position (1-based). This is used to compare against
the container's current step to decide if it's "complete",
"current", or "upcoming".

properties.name: lockBack
properties.description:
NEW: Lock API to disable users from going back.
When set to true, clicking on a previous (completed) step is ignored.

properties.name: status
properties.description:
Compute visual status for styling purposes. Note that if a step is explicitly
marked as completed, it always appears as complete even if it's active.

attributes.name: gap
attributes.description: Gap between steps. Maps to Tailwind gap classes.

properties.name: gap
properties.description: Gap between steps. Maps to Tailwind gap classes.

tags.name: schmancy-surface
tags.description:
`<schmancy-surface>` component

This component renders a styled container that adapts its dimensions based on the `fill` property.
It supports various rounding options, elevation levels, and applies background and text color classes
based on the specified surface variant. Additionally, when the `scroller` property is true, the component
enables internal scrolling by applying overflow and scroll-behavior styles.

SurfaceMixin automatically provides surfaceStyles CSS.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
Specifies the surface type for styling.
Provided to descendant components via context.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Specifies the surface type for styling.
Provided to descendant components via context.

slots.description: Default slot for projecting child content.

tags.name: schmancy-theme
tags.description:
SchmancyThemeComponent - Provides theming capabilities for Schmancy components.

This component manages color schemes, primary colors, and theme distribution
throughout the component tree. It can be used at the root level or nested
to provide different themes to different parts of the application.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Primary color for the theme in hex format.

attributes.name: root
attributes.description: Whether this theme should be applied at the root level (document.body).

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
Unique name for this theme instance (used for session storage).
If not provided, will be generated from DOM path.

attributes.name: theme
attributes.description: Theme configuration object containing all theme variables.

attributes.name: locale
attributes.description: Locale for number/date formatting. Overrides navigator.language when set.

properties.name: color
properties.description: Primary color for the theme in hex format.

properties.name: scheme
properties.description: Color scheme for the theme.

properties.description: Whether this theme should be applied at the root level (document.body).

Unique name for this theme instance (used for session storage).
If not provided, will be generated from DOM path.

properties.name: theme
properties.description: Theme configuration object containing all theme variables.

properties.name: locale
properties.description: Locale for number/date formatting. Overrides navigator.language when set.

properties.name: stableId
properties.description: Stable ID from DOM path - lazy, only computed on first access

properties.name: uid
properties.description:
Unique instance ID - can be overridden via attribute, otherwise auto-generated.
Usage: <my-component uid="custom-id"> or let it auto-generate

attributes.name: token
attributes.deprecatedMessage:
Prefer using Tailwind responsive text classes for better responsive design.
Set token="" and use class="text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg" instead.
Example: <schmancy-typography type="display" token="" class="text-2xl sm:text-3xl md:text-4xl">

attributes.description: When true, the element becomes contenteditable and dispatches 'change' events on blur/Enter
